role: Co-Founder and CEO of Blockstream, inventor of Hashcash
snippet: props to @Truthcoin and team for implementing and validating drivechain design.

role: Co-Founder and CEO of Blockstream, inventor of Hashcash
snippet: Drivechains...are pretty cool...and arguably could have been more important or useful than let’s say Taproot.

role: Co-Founder and CEO of Blockstream, inventor of Hashcash
snippet: I think we 're gonna need a big p2p sidechain for the next billion users to benefit from unseizable, censor-resistant #bitcoin. opcodes to support p2p sidechains like drivechain @LayerTwoLabs, p2p @liquid, @rootstock_io rootstock.

role: Co-founder ZeroSync, inventor of BitVM and BitStream
snippet: The brain drain is real though. I know dozens of bright researchers and engineers who left the bitcoin community because it takes more than a decade of pointless drama to activate even the most simple updates like covenants. We should activate BIP300 and then ossify the baselayer

role: author of Mastering the Lightning Network
snippet: On the weekend I watched this very interesting and insightful @BTCTKVR episode with @Truthcoin and @TheVladCostea https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RdyoOO_i7lk (2.5 hours) I find it sad that the bip300 discussion became so polarized as Paul has some strong arguments and meritfull observations. That being said: Recommending a pocast episode and encouraging dialog is no endorsement for or against bip300 as I do not really understand the implications of the proposal as my technical understanding of #Bitcoin is way to small to have a properly informed opinion.

role: creator of Nostr
snippet: Drivechains solve many different problems

role: creator of Nostr
snippet: However everything would be less stupid, and the arguments would be sounder, and the entire thing would work better if Bitcoin had drivechains and could scale properly. Maximalists would be happier and the non-scammy shitcoiners would be happier.

role: creator of Nostr
snippet: Drivechain is the only way.

role: CEO of Bitcoin.com
snippet: Paul is maybe one of the smartest people in Bitcoin... if there 's anyone can help Bitcoin scale, I do believe that some of his solutions may be a path forward...I 'm keeping a very close eye on Bip300 and 301

role: inventor of hosted channels and creator of the Simple Bitcoin Wallet, the Bitcoin Lightning Wallet, the Lightning Network Wallet, and the Scala IMMORTAN library
snippet: I fully support Drivechain because I want Hivemind since about 2018 when I first heard about them. In 2023 I want to add the following: neither hard-fork (obviously) nor a series of half-assed soft-forks will do. Soft-forks are not free, they add complexity to software code in nothing else. Throw 2-3 more soft-forks of Segwit scale and no one could be bothered figuring out what is going on there. We need one more soft-fork to end all soft-forks which is Drivechain. Another option is change nothing at all but it is a fragile solution which leaves BTC vulnerable to unknown future challenges and does not seem like an attractive investment offer to me.

role: CTO at Shining Block mining, and Software Engineer for the 2014 NYC Bitcoin Center
snippet: Drivechain is one of the boldest proposals out there. It 's not simple. It 's not perfect. But it gives us a real shot at scaling Bitcoin and evolving it without fragmenting the base layer or requiring constant forks.

role: freelance software developer specializing in Bitcoin, Lightning, and Nostr
snippet: I think we can probably do better than LN, and drivechain is one avenue toward better things.

role: Founder of LNBits
snippet: With BIP300 #LightningNetwork can have the "ideal "chain beneath it, making channel creation extremely cheap and viable enough for world adoption.

role: Host of the legendary Bitcoin Uncensored podcast
snippet: We need either Drivechain or something like it.

role: creator of Blixt Lightning Wallet
snippet: I support Drivechain (BIP 300 and 301)

role: Bitcoin Core developer
snippet: Merge mining is possible today. Custodial sidechains are possible today. IF there 's a harmful side effect of drivechains, it 's already here.

role: Co-founder of TABConf, Founder of The Atlanta BidDevs, and VP of Infrastructure at ZEBEDEE
snippet: I support Drivechain (BIP 300 and 301).

role: Chief Executive Officer of Bitcoin Magazine and The Bitcoin Conference
snippet: BIP 300 would improve Bitcoin builder culture infinitely. It would end 90% of the politics, new BIPS could be tried as a drivechain without changing base layer. Developers and new ideas would 1000x

role: Bitcoin educator and marketing advisor @trustmachinesco. Previously: Growth/Product @Kraken, @Uber, @Blockchain
snippet: ...The more speculative assets on Bitcoin the better as those assets introduce new audiences to Bitcoin. I observed this phenomenon with ETH at NFT NYC... Those concerned with MEV risk are simply avoiding the inevitable.

role: Bitcoin educator and marketing advisor @trustmachinesco. Previously: Growth/Product @Kraken, @Uber, @Blockchain
snippet: The narrative that people are “rushing” BIP 300 (Drivechains) is patently false. Folks have been working on sidechains and drivechains for ~8 years. I haven’t decided if I’m for or againat Drivechains, but the “rushing” narrative is a dishonest framing.

role: Host of the Bitcoin Takeover Podcast
snippet: Merged mining is a pretty big deal, yes. It 's also why I support the idea of Drivechains.

role: Co-founder at Bare Bitcoin
snippet: We desperately need to take a long and hard look in the mirror, and start doing things differently. I think starting a more serious and thorough discussion about BIP300 (Drivechain) is the right place to start.

role: Editor of Bitcoin Magazine and Editor-At-Large at Kraken Digital Asset Exchange
snippet: ...I think anybody in the developer community will tell you, even if they don 't want to do Drivechains, that they respect Paul Sztorc, because Paul Sztorc is trying to further Bitcoin as a technology.

role: CEO of Chainstone Labs, Host of the Satoshi Roundtable, and GOP Senate Candidate
Cheerleader-In-Chief at Sovryn
snippet: The only thing missing from Bitcoin is that ability to adopt any feature or technology without changing main chain. BIP 300 fixes this.

role: Host of the Bitcoin Audible podcast
snippet: I feel like sidechains are an inevitable part of the ecosystem for multiple reasons, &I simply see BIP300 as a hashrate based sidechain rather than a federated multisig. I 100% agree on the alts issue, but that 's not why i am interested in it.

role: Co-founder and Chief Technology Officer at Casa
snippet: I 'm generally in favor of finally fulfilling the promise of 2-way pegged sidechains. I 've yet to see a convincing concern of how it could be dangerous to the main chain. Some folks have said it could be dangerous if a sidechain became more valuable than the base chain, but seems to me that would be a signal that perhaps the base chain should implement that sidechain 's features.

role: Product at Sovryn, Bitcoin researcher, creator of www.BitcoinRollups.com
snippet: I support activating BIP300 as soon as the implementation is ready (dev 'd, reviewed, QAed, merged) and letting users decide when mining is "decentralized enough "for drivechains to be secure. Maybe it already is! Maybe it never will be! Only one way to know for sure: try it out.

role: Chief Executive Officer at ZuckBucks, Bitcoiner, entrepreneur, consultant and educator
snippet: I like drivechain but I 'm skeptical they will ever have consensus

role: Chief Executive Officer at ZuckBucks, Bitcoiner, entrepreneur, consultant and educator
snippet: I 'm not very convinced abour drivechains, I do like hashrate escrows, but I like status quo as default in case of any change less than obviously necessary.

role: Chief Executive Officer at ZuckBucks, Bitcoiner, entrepreneur, consultant and educator
snippet: Drivechains give lots of censoring power to miners. I 'd prefer a coinwitness kind of 2WP. But yes: a monero-like sidechain would be way better than Monero as a separate shitcoin, also for swaps (no slippage, less liquidity bottleneck).

role: Associate Professor of Humanities/Philosophy, Yale-NUS College
snippet: I support Drivechain (BIP 300 and 301)

role: Professor of Philosophy and Humanities at Reed College
snippet: [...] long-term we need either a better fee market, something like drivechains, or else… mining at a loss by large stakeholders.

role: Host of the Magic Internet Money podcast and Bitcoin-focused investor
snippet: Down the line when Bip 300 is activated, we can have more trustless sidechains.

role: Founder at SimpDAO
snippet: it’s been 7 years since @Truthcoin wrote the original drivechain blog post. if you went back in time and told me that 7 years later no effort has been made towards merge-mined sidechains but that the same people who opposed them were upset ethereum existed i’d laugh in your face

role: Founder at SimpDAO
snippet: I am still a drivechain supporter.

role: Founder and General Partner at Castle Island Ventures
snippet: I agree with all of this

role: Founder and Chief Investment Officer at BlockTower Capital
snippet: ...Drivechains are at least 4 years old. The LN whitepaper was 2016. If there had been aggressive innovation (from the user functionality perspective) on this stuff, I 'd agree it would have taken the wind out of many altcoin sails, but there wasn 't so it didn 't.

role: Host of TheOrdinalShow, Investor in 50+ BTC startups, Chief Executive Officer at Ninjalerts, General Partner at BTCFrontierFund
snippet: The most bullish thing about Bitcoin 's BIP 300 is seeing all the left curve Laser Eyes come out against it

role: cryptocurrency trader
snippet: As a self-professed Bitcoin maximalist, I support #BIP300. Enabling permissionless innovation on top of a stable and conservative Bitcoin base layer (rather than pushing them into shitcoinosphere) is a very important goalz. That 's how modern versatile Internet came into being.

role: Co-founder at EdgeWallet
snippet: I 'm a fan.

role: Lead Systems Architect at PrasosLtd
snippet: I don 't really see an argument against drivechain here. If 51% of miners are colluding hard enough and "willing to steal ", Bitcoin is in deep trouble, drivechains or not.

role: Founder and Chief Executive Officer at Satoshium, Inventor of Google Auto-Complete
snippet: As for Drivechains, DCs simply multiply usefulness of Bitcoin WITHOUT any risk to those who don 't want to use them.

role: Senior Researcher and Group Leader at Huawei (Machine Learning, Neuromorphic Computing, and Computational Neuroscience)
snippet: Any sidechain replacing an altcoin increases BTC 's security by 1. eliminating competitors with potential attack interests, 2. increasing the value of the token in which btc miners are paid. On top, 3. Drivechain brings more fees to btc miners, not changing mainchain fees or size.

role: Covenants Genius and Proponent of LNHance
snippet: some think giving a blanket permission for people to do anything on top of bitcoin is a bad idea. i see that as the main value proposition of dc, to get rid of fork campaigns and shouting matches on twitter. which means it all boils down where people will put their money?

role: Chief of Innovation at IOV Labs and designer of the RSK Rootstock Bitcoin sidechain
snippet: The idea to migrate Rootstock to a drivechain when it is softforked into Bitcoin is right in the original white paper. Anyone that takes part in Rootstock knows that the destiny is to become fully decentralized.

role: Co-founder at RSK Rootstock Bitcoin sidechain
snippet: Absolutely we count on the Bitcoin Core Devs to implement the Drivechain softfork as soon as possible to make Sidechains 2.0 a reality!

role: CEO and Co-founder at Luxor Technologies
snippet: Yes "[in response to "would you signal to activate BIP300?"]

role: Head of Architecture at Rhodium Enterprises, Inc.
snippet: ...my opinion on Drivechains. Here it is. In general I think it 's a good idea...

role: Bitcoin Sorcerer and creator of Spacechains
snippet: I have no strong stance on drivechains, for or against

role: Bitcoin Sorcerer and creator of Spacechains
snippet: Rollups are less scalable and more trustless (if implemented correctly) than drivechains.

role: Bitcoin Sorcerer and creator of Spacechains
snippet: Spacechains can at least allow for a subset of use cases that drivechains enable, but not all. If drivechains actually do work safely (big if), that would be ideal.

role: Founder of The Bitcoin Foundation, Host of the UntoldStories podcast
snippet: Yes people do and I support bip300 right now.

role: journalist at Bitcoin Magazine
snippet: Surely you wouldn 't prefer off-chain custodial accounts (eg. Coinbase) over sidechain balances (eg. Drivechain)?

role: Bitcoiner
snippet: I support BIP 300 and BIP 301!

role: Open Source Dev, Lightning Engineer at FoundryServices
snippet: I support Drivechains! (BIP 300/301)

role: Co-founder and Chief Executive Officer @GeyserFund
snippet: Sometimes I think the name "drivechains "drives people away. If you do the research, it makes so much sense to do drivechains. the main risk is for for the bitcoins on the drivechains that could be hijacked by miners. Big deal.

role: @bitcoinheiros
snippet: Drivechains - Crypto without shitcoins. Make Crypto Great Again

role: Host of The Stewart Mackenzie Indaba
snippet: The sooner BIP-300/1 happens the better.

role: Founder @interlayHQ
snippet: ...make BTC interoperability easier (BIP300 or similar)...

role: Ordinals Enthusiast/Influencer
snippet: *If* BIP300/301 *could* fox it then there is *at least* one fix that doesn 't require a modification of the supply cap.

role: financial privacy extremist
snippet: The goal should be to make bitcoin better, not try to "kill "other projects. Being scared of competition is a pussy move. This is why so many bitcoiners want other useful coins to fail, so that Bitcoin can be mediocre and still succeed. Bring on BIP300. make bitcoin good again.

role: creator of Bitcoin STAMPS
snippet: I don’t think we would see so much economic activity on other chains if we had Drivechain already.

role: Global Lead at Selligent Cortex
snippet: Drivechains are going to be the "Internet moment "of bitcoin. Instead of everyone building his own Ethernet network, everything is suddenly connected together. It will massively increase the value proposition of bitcoin, and as such, I am massively in favor of BIP 300/301.
